tasks.title: Initialize Expo project with TypeScript, ESLint, Prettier
tasks.body:
## Description
Set up a new Expo project with React Native 0.73+, TypeScript, and development tooling. Configure ESLint with React Native rules, Prettier for code formatting, and establish the base folder structure outlined in the architecture.

Create or update the following configuration files:
- app.json: Expo configuration with iOS 14.0+ and Android 11.0+ targets
- package.json: Dependencies for Expo SDK, React Navigation, AsyncStorage, Gesture Handler, TypeScript
- tsconfig.json: Strict mode enabled, target ES2020
- .eslintrc.json: React Native + React recommended rules
- .prettierrc.json: Standard formatting (2-space indent, single quotes)
- .gitignore: Standard Node.js/Expo ignores

## Estimated Files
- app.json
- package.json
- tsconfig.json
- .eslintrc.json
- .prettierrc.json
- .gitignore
- src/ (create folder structure)

## Dependencies
None (Wave 1 start)

## Parallelizable With
TASK-002, TASK-003

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] npm install completes without errors or warnings
- [ ] Running expo start launches the app successfully on Expo Go
- [ ] ESLint and Prettier run without configuration errors
- [ ] TypeScript compile check (tsc --noEmit) passes
- [ ] Folder structure (src/, __tests__/, etc.) exists and is ready for files

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md
- docs/requirements/spec_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
None

## Complexity
S
acceptance_criteria: npm install completes without errors or warnings
Running expo start launches app successfully
ESLint and Prettier run without configuration errors
TypeScript compile check (tsc --noEmit) passes

tasks.title: Define TypeScript types for Game, Player, Touch, and Navigation
tasks.body:
## Description
Create TypeScript interface definitions for all core data types used across the application. These types are the single source of truth for data structures passed between components, services, and context.

Define the following types:

**src/types/Game.ts:**
```typescript
export interface Player {
  id: string;  // UUID
  name: string;  // 1-50 chars
  score: number;  // -999999 to 999999
}

export interface Game {
  id: string;  // UUID
  name?: string;  // Optional user-provided name
  createdAt: number;  // Timestamp (milliseconds)
  lastModified: number;  // Timestamp (milliseconds)
  players: Player[];
}
```

**src/types/Touch.ts:**
```typescript
export interface Touch {
  id: string;  // Unique touch identifier
  x: number;  // X coordinate
  y: number;  // Y coordinate
  timestamp: number;  // Last update time
  color: string;  // Assigned color for circle
}
```

**src/types/Navigation.ts:**
```typescript
export type RootStackParamList = {
  Home: undefined;
  Dice: undefined;
  ScoreHome: undefined;
  ScoreGame: { gameId: string };
  Lottery: undefined;
};
```

## Estimated Files
- src/types/Game.ts
- src/types/Player.ts
- src/types/Touch.ts
- src/types/Navigation.ts
- src/types/index.ts (barrel export)

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001

## Parallelizable With
TASK-003

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All interface definitions compile without TypeScript errors
- [ ] Game.ts includes Player interface with id, name, score fields
- [ ] Touch.ts includes Touch interface with id, x, y, timestamp, color fields
- [ ] Navigation.ts includes RootStackParamList with all five routes
- [ ] index.ts barrel export allows import from src/types

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md
- docs/requirements/spec_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
None

## Complexity
S
acceptance_criteria: All TypeScript interfaces compile without errors
Game interface has id, name, createdAt, lastModified, players fields
Player interface has id, name, score fields with correct types
Touch interface has id, x, y, timestamp, color fields
Navigation types include all five routes: Home, Dice, ScoreHome, ScoreGame, Lottery

tasks.title: Create design system with theme, colors, spacing, and typography
tasks.body:
## Description
Establish a centralized design system (design tokens) that all screens and components use for consistent visual appearance. Implement responsive scaling utilities and WCAG AA compliant color palette.

**src/styles/theme.ts:** Define color palette (primary, secondary, success, error, backgrounds, text) with WCAG AA contrast ratios. Define typography scale (h1, h2, body, caption). Define spacing scale (4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, etc.).

**src/styles/responsive.ts:** Create useWindowDimensions wrapper to provide breakpoints (isSmallPhone: <360dp, isPhone: <600dp, isTablet: >=600dp). Export responsive utility functions for conditional font sizes and padding.

**src/utils/colors.ts:** Define color palette used across app: primary colors, semantic colors (success, error, warning), Lottery circle colors (8 distinct colors meeting WCAG AA). Include contrast ratio validation utilities.

**src/styles/globalStyles.ts:** Define reusable StyleSheet objects for common patterns (flexCenter, safeAreaContainer, cardStyle, buttonStyle). These are used by components.

## Estimated Files
- src/styles/theme.ts
- src/styles/responsive.ts
- src/styles/globalStyles.ts
- src/utils/colors.ts
- src/utils/accessibility.ts (accessibility utilities stub)

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001

## Parallelizable With
TASK-002

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Theme exports colors object with primary, secondary, background, text, success, error keys
- [ ] All text colors pass WCAG AA contrast check (4.5:1 for normal text) against background colors
- [ ] Responsive utility provides breakpoints for phone (<600dp), tablet (>=600dp)
- [ ] Lottery colors object exports 8 distinct colors, each passing WCAG AA
- [ ] globalStyles exports StyleSheet with flexCenter, safeAreaContainer, cardStyle patterns
- [ ] responsive.ts exports useResponsiveSizes hook returning { fontSize, padding, breakpoint }

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md
- docs/requirements/spec_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
None

## Complexity
S
acceptance_criteria: Theme exports colors object with all required keys (primary, secondary, background, text, success, error)
All text/background color combinations pass WCAG AA contrast check (4.5:1)
Colors.ts exports 8 distinct Lottery colors, each WCAG AA compliant
Responsive utilities provide correct breakpoints for phone and tablet
globalStyles exports reusable StyleSheet patterns
useResponsiveSizes hook returns fontSize and padding based on screen width

tasks.title: Set up React Navigation stack with RootNavigator
tasks.body:
## Description
Implement React Navigation v6+ with a native stack navigator for the five main screens. Create the root navigator component and App.tsx that wraps the navigation with Context providers for state management.

**src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx:**
- Create native stack navigator with five routes: Home, Dice, ScoreHome, ScoreGame (with gameId param), Lottery
- Configure screen options (header styling, animations)
- Set Home as initial route

**src/App.tsx:**
- Import RootNavigator
- Set up React Context providers (will be populated by TASK-007 and TASK-008)
- Wrap with NavigationContainer from @react-navigation/native
- Configure navigation linking for deep linking (optional for v1 but architecture-ready)

## Estimated Files
- src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx
- src/navigation/types.ts (already in types, but include screen name union)
- src/App.tsx

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001, TASK-002

## Parallelizable With
None (modifies App.tsx)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RootNavigator exports a functional component using createNativeStackNavigator
- [ ] Navigator includes five screens with correct route names and parameters
- [ ] App.tsx wraps RootNavigator with NavigationContainer and Provider stubs
- [ ] TypeScript compilation passes; RootStackParamList used for type safety
- [ ] Navigation transitions work: tapping home buttons navigates to respective screens

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
- src/types/Navigation.ts (reference in RootNavigator)

## Complexity
S
acceptance_criteria: RootNavigator uses createNativeStackNavigator with 5 screens
All routes typed correctly using RootStackParamList
ScoreGame route accepts gameId parameter
App.tsx wraps navigator with NavigationContainer
Navigation transitions between Home and all feature screens work correctly

tasks.title: Implement AsyncStorage service layer (AsyncStorageService, GameRepository)
tasks.body:
## Description
Create a storage abstraction layer that encapsulates all AsyncStorage operations for games and session state. This decouples business logic from storage implementation, allowing future migration to SQLite without changing the context layer.

**src/services/storage/AsyncStorageService.ts:**
Interface for key-value operations:
```typescript
export class AsyncStorageService {
  static async setItem(key: string, value: string): Promise<void>
  static async getItem(key: string): Promise<string | null>
  static async removeItem(key: string): Promise<void>
  static async clear(): Promise<void>  // For testing
}
```

**src/services/storage/GameRepository.ts:**
Game-specific repository:
```typescript
export class GameRepository {
  static async saveGames(games: Game[]): Promise<void>
  static async loadGames(): Promise<Game[]>
  static async saveLastGameId(gameId: string): Promise<void>
  static async loadLastGameId(): Promise<string | null>
  static async deleteLastGameId(): Promise<void>
}
```

Implementation details:
- Use @react-native-async-storage/async-storage package
- Keys: @games (array), @lastActiveGameId (string)
- JSON serialization/deserialization in repository
- Error handling: return empty array on load error, log errors

## Estimated Files
- src/services/storage/AsyncStorageService.ts
- src/services/storage/GameRepository.ts
- src/services/index.ts (barrel export)

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001, TASK-002

## Parallelizable With
TASK-006, TASK-007, TASK-008

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AsyncStorageService.setItem(key, value) persists string to device storage
- [ ] AsyncStorageService.getItem(key) retrieves persisted value or null if not found
- [ ] GameRepository.saveGames(games) JSON-serializes array and saves to @games key
- [ ] GameRepository.loadGames() deserializes JSON and returns Game[] (empty if not found)
- [ ] GameRepository.loadLastGameId() returns UUID string or null
- [ ] All methods handle AsyncStorage errors gracefully (no unhandled rejections)

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
None

## Complexity
S
acceptance_criteria: AsyncStorageService persists and retrieves string values correctly
GameRepository.saveGames serializes Game[] to JSON and saves atomically
GameRepository.loadGames deserializes and returns Game[] or [] if not found
GameRepository correctly handles and returns last game ID
All async operations handle errors without throwing unhandled rejections
Empty/missing data returns sensible defaults, not errors

tasks.title: Implement GameStateContext with useGameState hook
tasks.body:
## Description
Create a React Context that manages the global game state: list of saved games and the current active game. Implement useGameState hook to expose state and action methods to components. This is the single source of truth for all game data.

**src/context/GameStateContext.tsx:**
Context with the following interface:
```typescript
interface GameStateContextValue {
  games: Game[]
  currentGame: Game | null
  loading: boolean
  error: string | null
  createGame: (playerNames: string[]) => Promise<void>
  updateScore: (gameId: string, playerId: string, newScore: number) => Promise<void>
  resumeGame: (gameId: string) => Promise<void>
  deleteGame: (gameId: string) => Promise<void>
  clearCurrentGame: () => void
  loadLastSession: () => Promise<void>
}
```

**src/context/hooks/useGameState.ts:**
- Export hook to access context: useGameState() => GameStateContextValue

Implementation:
- createGame: Generates UUID, initializes players at 0 score, saves via GameRepository
- updateScore: Finds player in currentGame, updates score, triggers persistence hook
- resumeGame: Loads game from games array, sets as currentGame
- deleteGame: Removes from games array, updates storage, clears currentGame if it matches
- loadLastSession: Called on app init; loads last active game if it exists

## Estimated Files
- src/context/GameStateContext.tsx
- src/context/hooks/useGameState.ts

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001, TASK-002, TASK-005

## Parallelizable With
TASK-008, TASK-009, TASK-010

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GameStateContext provider accepts children and provides value to descendants
- [ ] useGameState hook returns games array, currentGame, and action methods
- [ ] createGame generates UUID, initializes all players to 0 score
- [ ] updateScore modifies correct player's score in currentGame
- [ ] deleteGame removes game from games array and clears currentGame if it was active
- [ ] resumeGame loads game and sets it as currentGame
- [ ] Context wraps calls to GameRepository for persistence

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
- src/App.tsx (wrap with GameStateProvider)

## Complexity
M
acceptance_criteria: GameStateContext exports provider component and context
useGameState hook returns all required state and action methods
createGame(playerNames) creates Game with UUID, timestamp, initialized players
updateScore(gameId, playerId, newScore) updates correct player in games array
resumeGame(gameId) loads game and sets as currentGame
deleteGame(gameId) removes game and clears currentGame if it was active
All actions trigger persistence via GameRepository

tasks.title: Implement usePersistence hook for debounced AsyncStorage writes
tasks.body:
## Description
Create a custom hook that monitors GameStateContext state changes and automatically persists the games array to AsyncStorage with debouncing. This implements FR-S7 (automatic persistence after every score change) without excessive I/O.

**src/context/hooks/usePersistence.ts:**
```typescript
export function usePersistence(games: Game[], lastGameId: string | null, debounceMs: number = 500): void
```

Behavior:
- Monitor games array changes
- Debounce with 500ms default (configurable)
- On debounce timeout, call GameRepository.saveGames(games)
- On mount, load games and lastGameId from storage
- Handle errors gracefully (log only, no UI errors)
- Cancel pending timeout on unmount

This hook is used in GameStateContext to persist state changes automatically.

## Estimated Files
- src/context/hooks/usePersistence.ts

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001, TASK-005

## Parallelizable With
TASK-007, TASK-009, TASK-010

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] usePersistence takes games array and returns void
- [ ] Hook uses useEffect to monitor games changes
- [ ] Debouncing prevents multiple writes within 500ms window
- [ ] Final write calls GameRepository.saveGames(games) with complete array
- [ ] Hook cleans up timeout on unmount
- [ ] No unhandled promise rejections if AsyncStorage fails
- [ ] Loading games on mount is non-blocking (async effect)

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
- src/context/GameStateContext.tsx (integrate this hook)

## Complexity
S
acceptance_criteria: usePersistence accepts games array and debounceMs parameter
Hook monitors games array via useEffect
Debouncing prevents writes if games changes multiple times within 500ms
Final write calls GameRepository.saveGames(games) with full array
Timeout is cleaned up on unmount (no memory leaks)
AsyncStorage errors are logged but do not crash app
Initial load from AsyncStorage is non-blocking

tasks.title: Create shared components (Button, Card, TextInput, ScreenContainer)
tasks.body:
## Description
Implement reusable, accessible component library used across all screens. These components enforce consistent styling, touch targets, and accessibility labels.

**src/components/common/Button.tsx:**
- Props: onPress, label, variant ('primary'|'secondary'|'danger'), size ('small'|'medium'|'large'), disabled
- Minimum 48×48 dp touch area
- Uses theme colors
- AccessibilityRole: 'button', accessibilityLabel
- Active state feedback (opacity change)

**src/components/common/TextInput.tsx:**
- Props: value, onChangeText, placeholder, maxLength, keyboardType
- Minimum 44×44 dp touch area
- Visible focus state (border highlight)
- AccessibilityRole: 'search' or 'none'

**src/components/common/Card.tsx:**
- Simple container with theme background, border radius, shadow
- Props: children, onPress (optional for tappable cards)

**src/components/common/ScreenContainer.tsx:**
- Wraps screen content with safe area, consistent padding
- Props: children, noHorizontalPadding

## Estimated Files
- src/components/common/Button.tsx
- src/components/common/TextInput.tsx
- src/components/common/Card.tsx
- src/components/common/ScreenContainer.tsx
- src/components/common/index.ts (barrel export)

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001, TASK-003

## Parallelizable With
TASK-007, TASK-008, TASK-009

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Button component has minimum 48×48 dp touch area
- [ ] Button has active state visual feedback (opacity/color change)
- [ ] TextInput has minimum 44×44 dp touch area and visible focus state
- [ ] All components use colors from theme
- [ ] All interactive components have accessibilityLabel and accessibilityRole
- [ ] ScreenContainer respects safe area (notch, Dynamic Island)
- [ ] Components render correctly in both portrait and landscape

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
None

## Complexity
M
acceptance_criteria: Button component has minimum 48×48 dp touch target size
Button provides active state feedback (color/opacity change)
TextInput has minimum 44×44 dp touch area and visible focus state
All components use colors from theme with correct contrast ratios
Interactive components include accessibilityLabel and accessibilityRole
ScreenContainer respects safe area padding
All components render correctly in portrait and landscape orientations

tasks.title: Implement Dice screen, components, and rolling logic
tasks.body:
## Description
Create the Dice feature screen with dice count selector, roll animation, and results display.

**src/screens/dice/DiceScreen.tsx:**
- Display dice count selector (1-6 dice) with increment/decrement buttons
- "Roll" button that triggers DiceRoller.rollDice() and animation
- Animated dice display showing each die result
- Total sum display
- "Roll Again" or "Clear" button to reset

**src/components/dice/DiceCountSelector.tsx:**
- Increment/decrement buttons for dice count (1-6)
- Display current count

**src/components/dice/DiceDisplay.tsx:**
- Shows individual die results as numbers (1-6)
- Layout: horizontal or grid depending on count

**src/components/dice/RollAnimation.tsx:**
- Reusable animated spinning effect using Animated API
- Duration: 0.5-1.5 seconds
- Shows all dice spinning, then settles on final values

Logic:
- Use DiceRoller.rollDice(count) to get results
- Trigger RollAnimation on roll button tap
- Display results after animation completes
- State: diceCount, results, isAnimating

## Estimated Files
- src/screens/dice/DiceScreen.tsx
- src/components/dice/DiceCountSelector.tsx
- src/components/dice/DiceDisplay.tsx
- src/components/dice/RollAnimation.tsx

## Parallelizable With

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DiceScreen renders dice count selector (buttons to select 1-6)
- [ ] "Roll" button triggers animation and generates random results
- [ ] Animation runs for 0.5-1.5 seconds with spinning effect
- [ ] After animation, individual die results and sum are displayed
- [ ] User can roll again without changing dice count
- [ ] Touch targets (buttons, Roll) are minimum 48×48 dp
- [ ] Screen respects safe area and landscape orientation

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md
- docs/requirements/spec_0001.md

None (RootNavigator references this screen)

M
acceptance_criteria: DiceScreen displays dice count selector and Roll button
Selecting 1-6 dice updates the UI
Rolling generates random results for each die (1-6)
Roll animation plays for 0.5-1.5 seconds with spinning effect
Final results and sum are displayed after animation
User can roll again without changing count
All buttons are at least 48×48 dp
Screen works in portrait and landscape orientation

tasks.title: Implement Score home screen and game list display

tasks.title: Implement Score game screen with player rows and score controls
tasks.body:
## Description
Create the active game screen where players track scores during a game session. Display player names, current scores, and increment/decrement buttons.

**src/screens/score/ScoreGameScreen.tsx:**
- Display game.name at top
- List of all players with rows showing: name, current score, increment/decrement controls
- Score increment buttons: +1, +5, +10 (and optional manual entry)
- Score decrement buttons: −1, −5, −10
- "Back" or "Finish Game" button returns to ScoreHome (currentGame cleared)
- Pull-to-refresh or manual "Load" to sync with latest game state
- Uses useGameState() to access currentGame and updateScore

**src/components/score/PlayerRow.tsx:**
- Displays single player: name, score, increment/decrement buttons
- Buttons are large (48×48 dp) and clearly labeled
- Score updates immediately on button tap
- Accessible: labels for each button, clear text

**src/components/score/ScoreIncrement.tsx:**
- Reusable buttons for +1, +5, +10, −1, −5, −10
- Takes onPress(delta: number) callback
- Size and styling from theme

Logic:
- Load game on mount using route.params.gameId
- Call updateScore() on increment/decrement button tap
- Debounced persistence happens in GameStateContext via usePersistence

## Estimated Files
- src/screens/score/ScoreGameScreen.tsx
- src/components/score/PlayerRow.tsx
- src/components/score/ScoreIncrement.tsx

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001, TASK-003, TASK-004, TASK-007, TASK-010

## Parallelizable With
TASK-011, TASK-012, TASK-014, TASK-015, TASK-016, TASK-017

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ScoreGameScreen displays game name and all players with current scores
- [ ] Each player has +1, +5, +10 buttons (and −1, −5, −10 decrement buttons)
- [ ] Tapping increment/decrement button updates player score immediately in UI
- [ ] Score updates are automatically persisted (via GameStateContext)

tasks.title: Implement New Game dialog with player name input
tasks.body:

- "Start Game" button (disabled until at least 2 names filled in)
- "Cancel" button
- On "Start Game": Call createGame(playerNames), navigate to ScoreGame with gameId

Logic:
- State: playerCount, playerNames array

- Call useGameState().createGame(playerNames)
- Navigate to ScoreGame with returned game ID

tasks.title: Implement Lottery screen with multi-touch tracking and gesture handler
tasks.body:
## Description
Create the Lottery feature screen with full-screen touch detection and colored circle display for each simultaneous touch.

**src/screens/lottery/LotteryScreen.tsx:**
- Full-screen touch-responsive surface
- Uses React Native Gesture Handler for multi-touch tracking
- Detects up to 8 simultaneous touches
- On touch start/move/end: updates circles state
- Manages 3-second countdown timer
- On countdown completion: triggers LotteryEngine.selectWinner()

**src/components/lottery/TouchSurface.tsx:**
- Full-screen PanGestureHandler from Gesture Handler
- Tracks multiple touch points
- Calls callback with array of current touches
- Enforces max 8 touches (ignore 9th finger)

**src/components/lottery/TouchCircle.tsx:**
- Visual circle representing a touch point
- Props: x, y, color, isWinner, growing (for animation)
- Uses Animated API for growth and fade effects

Logic:
- Track active touches in state
- Monitor for 3 seconds of stability (no touch add/remove)
- Reset countdown if touches change
- Call selectWinner() after stability countdown completes

## Estimated Files
- src/screens/lottery/LotteryScreen.tsx
- src/components/lottery/TouchSurface.tsx
- src/components/lottery/TouchCircle.tsx

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001, TASK-003, TASK-004, React Native Gesture Handler

## Parallelizable With
TASK-011, TASK-012, TASK-013, TASK-014, TASK-015, TASK-017

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LotteryScreen displays full-screen touch surface
- [ ] Touching screen creates colored circles at touch points
- [ ] Up to 8 simultaneous touches are tracked and displayed
- [ ] 9th touch is ignored with visual feedback message
- [ ] Circles remain visible as long as fingers are on screen
- [ ] 3-second countdown begins after touches stabilize
- [ ] Countdown resets if touches change (add/remove)
- [ ] After 3 seconds, vibration occurs (TASK-017 integrates haptics)

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md
- docs/requirements/spec_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
None

## Complexity
M
acceptance_criteria: LotteryScreen displays full-screen touch surface
Each touch creates a colored circle at contact point
Up to 8 simultaneous touches are tracked
9th touch is rejected with visual feedback
Circles persist while fingers are on screen
3-second countdown timer starts after touches stabilize
Countdown resets if touches change (add/remove)
Countdown completion triggers winner selection (TASK-017)

export class LotteryEngine {

tasks.title: Implement session restoration on app launch (last active game)
tasks.body:
## Description
Implement automatic app startup logic to restore the last active game session per ADR-15. If the app is closed and reopened, users resume the game they were playing.

**src/services/storage/SessionManager.ts:**
```typescript
export class SessionManager {
  static async saveLastGameId(gameId: string): Promise<void>
  static async getLastGameId(): Promise<string | null>
  static async clearLastGameId(): Promise<void>
}
```

**src/context/hooks/useSessionRestore.ts:**
- Hook called on app initialization
- Loads last game ID from storage
- If ID exists and game is in games array, set as currentGame
- If ID exists but game was deleted (not in array), show toast: "Last game was deleted; here are your saved games"
- Navigate to ScoreGame or ScoreHome accordingly

Integration:
- Call useSessionRestore() on app mount in App.tsx or context initialization
- Update GameStateContext.resumeGame() to also call SessionManager.saveLastGameId()
- If game is deleted while active, fall back to ScoreHome (ADR-15)

## Estimated Files
- src/services/storage/SessionManager.ts
- src/context/hooks/useSessionRestore.ts
- A toast/notification component (or use simple Alert)

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001, TASK-002, TASK-005, TASK-007

## Parallelizable With
TASK-019

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] On app launch, useSessionRestore() loads last game ID from storage
- [ ] If game exists in games array, it is loaded as currentGame
- [ ] Navigation automatically opens ScoreGame with last game on startup
- [ ] If game was deleted, app falls back to ScoreHome and shows toast message
- [ ] Toast displays: "Last game was deleted; here are your saved games"
- [ ] SessionManager.saveLastGameId() is called whenever resumeGame() is called
- [ ] App startup time remains under 1 second (loading is non-blocking)

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
- src/App.tsx (call useSessionRestore on mount)
- src/context/GameStateContext.tsx (call SessionManager.saveLastGameId in resumeGame)

## Complexity
S
acceptance_criteria: useSessionRestore loads last game ID on app startup
If game exists in array, it becomes currentGame and opens ScoreGame
If game was deleted, app shows toast and navigates to ScoreHome
Toast message is clear: 'Last game was deleted; here are your saved games'
SessionManager.saveLastGameId is called after resumeGame
App startup is fast (<1 second including async load)

tasks.title: Accessibility audit and WCAG AA compliance fixes
tasks.body:
## Description
Perform comprehensive accessibility review of all screens and components against WCAG AA standards. Fix any violations before release.

Audit checklist:
- **Color Contrast (WCAG AA 4.5:1):** All text vs. background colors meet minimum ratio
- **Text Size:** Minimum 16sp for body text; support dynamic type scaling (text size settings)
- **Touch Targets (NFR-A2):** All interactive elements are at least 48×48 dp
- **Screen Reader Support (NFR-A5):** All interactive elements have accessibilityLabel, accessibilityRole, accessibilityHint
- **Focus Management:** Visual focus indicator on all interactive elements
- **Keyboard Navigation:** All features work via keyboard (if applicable to mobile context)
- **Color Not Only:** Lottery circles are distinguishable by color + position or label, not color alone
- **Motion:** Animations respect prefersReducedMotion setting
- **Form Labels:** Text inputs have clear labels, not just placeholders

Tooling:
- Use Accessible Colors tool to check contrast ratios
- Test with iOS VoiceOver and Android TalkBack screen readers
- Use Accessibility Inspector on iOS simulator
- Manual testing on real devices

Fixes:
- Update colors if contrast is below 4.5:1
- Add accessibilityLabel to all buttons, inputs, images
- Increase touch target sizes if below 48×48 dp
- Add accessibilityHint to buttons with unclear purpose
- Implement useColorScheme() for dark mode support (bonus)

## Estimated Files
Modifications across all component and screen files:
- src/screens/** (all)
- src/components/** (all)
- src/styles/theme.ts (verify contrast ratios)
- src/utils/accessibility.ts (add contrast validation utilities)

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-011, TASK-012, TASK-013, TASK-014, TASK-015, TASK-016, TASK-017

## Parallelizable With
None (touches all files)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All text colors pass WCAG AA contrast check (4.5:1 normal, 3:1 large text)
- [ ] All interactive elements are at least 48×48 dp touch area
- [ ] All buttons, inputs, links have accessibilityLabel and accessibilityRole
- [ ] VoiceOver on iOS can read and interact with all features
- [ ] TalkBack on Android can read and interact with all features
- [ ] Lottery circles are distinguishable without relying solely on color
- [ ] Text respects system Dynamic Type settings (iOS) and sp scaling (Android)
- [ ] Animations respect prefersReducedMotion setting

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md
- docs/requirements/spec_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
- All screen and component files (add accessibility attributes)
- src/styles/theme.ts (verify/fix colors)

## Complexity
L
acceptance_criteria: All text/background color combinations pass WCAG AA (4.5:1) contrast test
All interactive elements are at least 48×48 dp
All buttons, inputs have accessibilityLabel and accessibilityRole
VoiceOver (iOS) can read and interact with all features
TalkBack (Android) can read and interact with all features
Lottery circles are distinguishable without color alone
Text respects Dynamic Type (iOS) and sp scaling (Android)
Animations respect prefersReducedMotion system setting

- Create __tests__/ directory structure mirroring src/

**Test Files:**
- __tests__/context/GameStateContext.test.ts

  - Test updateScore updates player score
  - Test deleteGame removes game from array

- __tests__/services/AsyncStorageService.test.ts
  - Mock AsyncStorage
  - Test setItem/getItem round-trip
  - Test error handling
  
- __tests__/services/DiceRoller.test.ts

  - Test all values 1-6

- __tests__/services/LotteryEngine.test.ts

  - Test it doesn't always return the same touch
  
- __tests__/components/Button.test.tsx (minimal component test)
  - Test onPress callback is called
  - Test label is rendered

Target: >80% code coverage for services and context

tasks.title: Performance optimization and animations tuning
tasks.body:
## Description
Optimize app performance to meet NFR-P1 (60 FPS animations) and NFR-P3 (game data loads <500ms). Identify and fix performance bottlenecks.

**Optimizations:**
- Component memoization: useMemo, React.memo for components that receive complex props
- useCallback for event handlers to prevent unnecessary re-renders
- Remove unused dependencies from useEffect
- Test frame rate with React DevTools Profiler
- Optimize animations: use React Native Animated (already used) and ensure interpolations are smooth
- Lazy load screens (React.lazy + Suspense, optional for v1)
- Minimize re-renders of PlayerRow on score updates
- Profile AsyncStorage load time; add caching if needed

**Testing:**
- Use React DevTools Profiler to measure render times
- Use Expo's built-in Performance Monitor
- Test on low-end device simulator (Android 6, iPhone 6s equivalent)
- Ensure dice animation runs at 60 FPS
- Ensure circle animations run at 60 FPS
- Measure game load time: target <500ms
- Measure app startup time: target <2 seconds

**Changes:**
- Add React.memo to components that don't need frequent re-renders
- Add useCallback to event handlers in context
- Review and optimize animation interpolations
- Consider FlatList virtualization for game list if >100 games

## Estimated Files
- src/components/** (memoization)
- src/context/GameStateContext.tsx (useCallback)
- src/screens/dice/DiceScreen.tsx (animation optimization)
- src/screens/lottery/LotteryScreen.tsx (animation optimization)
- src/components/score/PlayerRow.tsx (memoization)

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-019 (needs all features built first)

## Parallelizable With
TASK-021, TASK-022

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dice rolling animation maintains 60 FPS on low-end device
- [ ] Lottery circle animations maintain 60 FPS
- [ ] Game data loads from AsyncStorage in <500ms
- [ ] App startup (root to Home screen) completes in <2 seconds
- [ ] PlayerRow re-renders only when its score changes (memoized)
- [ ] No console warnings about unnecessary renders
- [ ] React DevTools Profiler shows no excessive render times

## Context Files
- docs/architecture/architecture_approved_0001.md

## Existing Files to Modify
- All component and screen files (add memoization)
- src/context/GameStateContext.tsx (add useCallback)

## Complexity
M
acceptance_criteria: Dice rolling animation maintains 60 FPS on low-end device (Pixel 3 equivalent)
Lottery circle animations maintain 60 FPS
Game data loads from AsyncStorage in <500ms
App startup completes in <2 seconds
PlayerRow only re-renders when its score changes (memoized)
No console warnings about excessive renders
React DevTools Profiler shows average render time <16ms
